en.extract:
The Penan bulbul (Alophoixus ruficrissus) is a species of songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in Borneo, typically in the mid-storey of broad-leaved evergreen and rainforests up to 1500 metres elevation.


Subspecies
Three subspecies are recognized:

A. r. fowleri - (Amadon & Harrisson, 1957): Found in montane areas of Borneo, except Sabah
A. r. meratusensis - Shakya et al., 2020: Found in mountains of south-eastern Borneo (Kalimantan)
A. r. ruficrissus - (Sharpe, 1879): Found in mountains of north-eastern Borneo (Sabah)


References

The '''Penan bulbul''' ('''''Alophoixus ruficrissus''''') is a species of [[songbird]] in the [[bulbul]] family, Pycnonotidae. It is found in [[Borneo]], typically in the mid-storey of broad-leaved evergreen and rainforests up to 1500 metres elevation.

== Subspecies ==
Three subspecies are recognized:<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/|title=Bulbuls « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-05-14}}</ref>
* ''A. r. fowleri'' - <small>([[Dean Amadon|Amadon]] & [[Tom Harrisson|Harrisson]], 1957)</small>: Found in montane areas of Borneo, except [[Sabah]]
* ''A. r. meratusensis'' - <small>Shakya et al., 2020</small>: Found in mountains of south-eastern Borneo (Kalimantan)
* ''A. r. ruficrissus'' - <small>([[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1879)</small>: Found in mountains of north-eastern Borneo (Sabah)
